channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he was being a 'bit sarcastic' when he promised to end Russia-Ukraine war in 24 hours
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday that he was “being a little bit sarcastic” when he repeatedly claimed as a candidate that he would have the Russia-Ukraine war solved within 24 hours — and even before he even took office.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most disapprove of Trump's approach to Ukraine, Russia: Survey
item.description: Nearly six in 10 people, 59 percent, said it was “not too" or “not at all likely” that Trump’s approach to the Russia-Ukraine war will bring long-term peace between the two Eastern European countries. Around 41 percent disagreed.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Rubio says South Africa’s ambassador to the US 'is no longer welcome' in the country
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Friday that South Africa’s ambassador to the United States “is no longer welcome” in the country, in the latest Trump administration move targeting the African nation.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Emerging from 2024 loss, Tim Walz holds a town hall in an Iowa Republican's district
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz is reemerging from a public-facing lull that followed last year’s election loss.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Fingerhut, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Infighting to Irrelevance
item.description: The current Democratic infighting and political malpractice over forcing or not forcing a government shutdown exposes a leaderless party that believes in opposing Donald Trump and issues that 80% of Americans agree with.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s paid leave law remains a flashpoint as launch nears
item.description: MPR News senior politics editor Brian Bakst talks with two Minnesota lawmakers about the state’s new paid leave program — scheduled to start in January of next year — and the legislation that could possibly delay or change eligibility.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Trump environmental rollbacks would boost pollution and endanger lives, former EPA heads say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Three former Environmental Protection Agency leaders sounded an alarm Friday, saying rollbacks proposed by EPA Administrator Lee Zeldin endanger the lives of millions of Americans and abandon the agency's dual mission to protect the environment and human health.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Gov. Hochul contacted NYC Council speaker before she announced mayoral run
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Among the calls New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams took before hopping into the race for mayor was from Gov. Kathy Hochul. "I heard from the governor," said Adrienne Adams.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Senate passes 6-month funding bill, sending measure to Trump’s desk
item.description: The Senate passed a six-month spending bill on Friday, hours before a government shutdown, overcoming sharp Democratic opposition to the measure and sending it to President Donald Trump to be signed into law.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Senate Democrats signal plans to advance GOP bill to avoid a government shutdown
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer, D-NY, said he plans to vote for a Republican bill to fund the government through September, paving the way for other Democrats to join him.
item.dc:creator: Deirdre Walsh, Claudia Grisales, and Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Trump pushes annexation of Greenland during meeting with NATO secretary
item.description: President Trump on Thursday expressed confidence the United States would annex Greenland, even suggesting the head of the NATO alliance could be a key player in facilitating the acquisition.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Dem AGs sue over Trump administration’s Education Department layoffs
item.description: Democratic attorneys general in Washington, D.C., and 20 states sued Thursday over the Trump administration’s efforts to lay off nearly half of the Education Department workforce.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Trump meets with NATO chief Mark Rutte
item.description: The meeting comes after Trump called on European allies to contribute more to the alliance's defense fund.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat trying again to secure discounts for all Nevadans on 10 expensive prescription medications
item.description: Prescription medication costs are forcing some hard choices for many Nevadans. Two years ago, a proposal that offered hope sailed through the Democrat-majority Nevada Legislature only to be vetoed by Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada cuts wait time for inmates needing mental health care
item.description: The wait time for inmates needing a mental health bed to regain competency was down to an average 74 days compared to 78 when the 8 News Now Investigators reported on the statewide problem last fall.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: U.S. inflation cooled last month, though trade war threatens to lift prices
item.description: U.S. inflation slowed last month for the first time since September even as additional tariffs on steel and aluminum kicked in Wednesday that could send prices higher.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: EPA signals it could narrow Clean Water Act protections
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has signaled that it could narrow which set of waters receive protections under the Clean Water Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Digital license plate bill could include a way for Nevadans to pay registration in installments
item.description: GPS features, no stickers and possibly a way to pay your vehicle registration in installments. Those were some of the reasons that digital license plates might get support at the Nevada Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's approval dips amid concerns over economy: Poll
item.description: President Donald Trump’s approval rating has dropped a few points since he first took office, with voters increasingly expressing concerns over the state of the economy, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, Fox 5

item.title: DUI punishments higher under 2 bills introduced Monday in Nevada Senate
item.description: Republicans are proposing more severe punishment for driving under the influence with two new bills introduced Monday in the Nevada Senate.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
